title: Project
description: the release unit — the foundation-cross-cutting reification of the thing that gets tagged, versioned, and shipped. Scopes a Workflow's execution to a specific repo + path location with declared version files, branch policy, and dependency edges. A Project comprises one or more Components — PD-domain implementation units — and is released by a Workflow referenced via release_workflow_ref. Projects optionally link to an external Product via belongs_to_product_ref string until cross-artifact ref resolution lands in v0.7. Three scenarios it enables: monorepo with multiple Projects under different Products each with its own release Workflow scoped via source.path; multi-repo with multiple Projects each its own repo composing one Product; shared library where a Project library is consumed by Projects in other Products and dependency edges drive orchestration. Backstage Catalog Component / System, Nx project, Lerna package, Bazel package, Cargo workspace member all model this primitive — adopted as the convention per CP-01.

source.description: JSON-encoded object with keys repo, path, primary_branch — see _v0_6_grammar_consideration for the future tagged-union shape

items.description: paths within source.path holding version strings

belongs_to_product_ref.description: string ULID of an external Product — domain-specific; PD Product or future foundation Product. Foundation cannot type-ref domain entities; cross-artifact ref resolution lands in v0.7+
